replacement_quality: POOR - >50 Ma younger; would push node min from 98 to ~37 Ma
notes: No compressional Cretaceous psephenid known outside Burmese amber; gap is substantial

non_burmese_alternative: Lycidae from Eocene Baltic amber (multiple spp.)
replacement_quality: POOR - >50 Ma younger; also Eocene Dominican amber (~16-20 Ma)
notes: Jurassic Lycidae unknown; Burmese amber is only Cretaceous Lycidae; complete loss of Mesozoic constraint

Firefly fossil record extremely sparse pre-Eocene; new 2024 study (Proc Royal Society B) described another Cretaceous lampyrid from Kachin amber but that is also Burmese; node would have no Mesozoic constraint

non_burmese_alternative: Osoriinae compression fossils from Yixian Formation; earliest is Cenomanian Baltic amber Osoriinae
replacement_quality: MODERATE for Yixian-age (if valid Osoriinae confirmed); POOR for Baltic amber alternative
notes: Yamamoto 2025 described Eleusinini from Hkamti amber (lower Cretaceous ~110 Ma); compressional Yixian record exists but needs phylogenetic placement confirmation; Baltic Eocene Osoriinae ~37 Ma would be a very poor substitute

replacement_quality: GOOD - approximately same age as Burmese amber; Albian/Cenomanian boundary
notes: Best non-Burmese replacement available; Charentes amber is latest Albian-earliest Cenomanian (~98 Ma); Stephanopachys vetus is assigned to extant subfamily Stephanopachyinae; both ambiguities in subfamily placement exist but calibration age effectively unchanged

non_burmese_alternative: Unnamed Ripiphoridae from Middle Jurassic Daohugou China; Mordellidae from Albian Spanish amber
alternative_paper: Hsiao et al. 2017 (Daohugou); Peris & Delclòs 2013 (Spanish amber)
replacement_quality: MODERATE for Jurassic Ripiphoridae (older but uncertain placement); GOOD for Albian Spanish amber Mordellidae (105 Ma)
notes: A Jurassic ripiphorid exists from Daohugou beds (~165 Ma); if phylogenetically validated this would provide an OLDER and non-Burmese constraint; Albian Spanish amber Mordellidae (105 Ma) would push node min slightly younger than Burmese alternative

non_burmese_alternative: Eocene Baltic amber Colydiinae (Xylolaemus Endophloeus Bitoma)
replacement_quality: POOR - ~50 Ma younger; no confirmed pre-Cenomanian non-Burmese Colydiinae
notes: Only Cretaceous Zopheridae are from Burmese amber; loss of this node yields a ~50 Ma shift in minimum age

non_burmese_alternative: No confirmed pre-Eocene non-Burmese Endomychidae known
replacement_quality: POOR - ~50 Ma younger; significant node loss
notes: Endomychidae fossil record is sparse; Eocene Oise French amber yields handsome fungus beetles but ~53 Ma; this would be the largest calibration gap if Burmese amber excluded

non_burmese_alternative: Eocene Baltic amber Silvanidae (Airaphilus Cathartosilvanus Silvanus)
notes: Silvanidae known only from Burmese amber and Eocene ambers in Cretaceous-Eocene window; all Eocene alternatives are much younger
